target.name: Astera Labs
target.founder: Jitendra Mohan, Sanjay Gajendra, Casey Morrison

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.1B - $39.2B.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $39.2B

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $2.26B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.09B

data.narrative:
Astera Labs is a public-stage semiconductor company founded by Jitendra Mohan, Sanjay Gajendra, and Casey Morrison, having raised $713M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 public peers within Chips & Semiconductors—Cerebras ($39.2B), Ouster ($2.3B), and Aeva Technologies ($1.1B)—supplemented by 8 private-stage frontier semiconductor firms for directional context.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given available data, yields a valuation of $2.3B. This median reflects the middle point across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables, with valuations ranging from $1.1B to $39.2B. The wide dispersion signals material heterogeneity in stage, capital intensity, and near-term path to profitability within the public semiconductor cohort, limiting the precision of peer-based inference.

Analysis supports a valuation range of $1.9B to $2.6B, centered on $2.3B. The median estimate remains most defensible, though the upper and lower bounds reflect measurement uncertainty rather than alternative methodological approaches. Key limitations include reliance on a single peer-median technique, the inherent volatility of public comparables in frontier semiconductors, and absence of revenue, margin, or growth rate normalization. Investors should weight this estimate against discounted cash flow or venture capital methods if available.
